summary: Excellent DTS soundboard recording of a legendary 1977 show, highlighted by soaring Jerry solos and a fire‑filled set featuring Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain and a stellar Morning Dew.
review: Fans rave about this crystal‑clear DTS 5.1 surround mix, calling it “amazing” and “over the top,” while the performance is described as one of the band’s best – with killer first set, a brilliant second set that flows from Scarlet Begonias into Fire on the Mountain, Jack Straw, St. Stephen/NFA/St. Stephen/Morning Dew and more. A few dissenting voices note slower tempos for the era, but overall the show is praised as a top‑tier Dead experience.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (10+) are 5‑star and praise both the performance and the high‑fidelity DTS soundboard mix; only a few lower‑rated comments mention tempo issues, so the weighted rating is very strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: 5.1 DTS surround mix (Betty Board) praised as crystal‑clear and immersive; requires proper DVD/CD player and receiver or software decoder, but delivers crisp, spacious audio with no reported dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans describe a killer first set and an especially brilliant second set with seamless transitions (Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain, then St. Stephen/NFA/St. Stephen/Morning Dew). The pacing is praised as energetic and ethereal, though one reviewer notes the era’s generally slower tempos.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Highly praised for his soaring, climbing solo on Morning Dew and overall guitar brilliance throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mixed remarks – some reviewers dismiss Bob as “sucks,” while others note his heartfelt ‘Thanks, Mom’ moment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Preference expressed for Brent Mydland over Keith Godchaux; no specific performance details beyond personal taste.
